page: Microsoft Azure D48ls_v5 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the DLSv5 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is configured with 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. No local storage is bundled with this instance, though it is premium storage capable, and there are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-threaded performance and efficient instruction execution. The instance achieves a Passmark CPU score of 48,476.63 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 221.23 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% tier. Cryptographic capabilities are also strong, with a Passmark CPU encryption test score of 43,318.73, aided by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA extensions. For memory-bound tasks, the system demonstrates a read-only small-block cached memory bandwidth of 2,520.48 GB/sec. Large-block uncached memory operations reach 164.15 GB/sec for reads and 70.68 GB/sec for writes.

This instance is highly suited for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints, such as multi-threaded data decompression, software compilation, and image processing. It also demonstrates strong performance in small-to-medium language model inference, processing 199.49 tokens per second for 7B model prompts. However, the 2.0 GB per vCPU memory allocation represents a tradeoff for memory-intensive database operations or large-scale caching layers. The lack of local storage requires relying on external premium storage volumes for persistent data.
description: The Microsoft Azure D48ls_v5 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the DLSv5 family, featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs and 96.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz, this x86_64 instance utilizes Microsoft Hyper-V virtualization. It lacks local storage but is premium storage capable. Performance benchmarks show strong capabilities in multi-threaded processing, with a Passmark CPU score of 48,476.63 and a bzip3 decompression speed of 221.23 GB/sec. It also excels in cryptographic tasks and small-to-medium LLM inference. With a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, this instance is highly cost-efficient for compute-heavy, memory-light workloads. It is best suited for software compilation, image processing, multi-threaded decompression, and medium-sized model inference tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48ls_v5 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96.0 GB of RAM, and strong multi-threaded performance for compilation and inference workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48ls_v5 is a compute-optimized VM with 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 96 GB RAM, built for multi-threaded and cryptographic workloads.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine delivering high multi-threaded performance and dedicated Intel Xeon processing for compute-intensive, low-memory workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz
96.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Strong multi-threaded performance in bzip3 decompression and image processing
Excellent cryptographic execution supported by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA
Optimized for software compilation, medium LLM inference, and compute-heavy tasks
Premium storage capable configuration with no bundled local storage
